description_long: This is a vintage-style van with a two-tone paint scheme, featuring a cream upper body and a blue lower body. The van has a boxy, utilitarian shape with rounded edges and a flat roof, typical of mid-20th-century delivery vehicles. The front end displays a split windshield, prominent chrome trim, and circular headlights, while the rear has a single large window and a two-part door. The side profile reveals two main doors at the front and a long, uninterrupted cargo area, with small, round side mirrors and classic steel wheels. The van's windows are blacked out, suggesting either tinted glass or a model representation. Its overall design is functional, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on cargo capacity.
description: A vintage, two-tone cream and blue van with a boxy shape, split windshield, and classic steel wheels. The design emphasizes utility and cargo space, with minimal decorative features.
description_view_0: Rear view showing a large rectangular window, two rear doors, and simple taillights.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the long cargo area, front door, and classic wheel design.
description_view_2: Front view featuring a split windshield, chrome trim, circular headlights, and a prominent grille.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with visible door seams and uninterrupted cargo panel.
attribute_door_configuration: two front doors, two rear doors

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
